archetype: Automated Fireworks Display Synchronization and Safety Monitoring System for Large-Scale Public Events
description: This system coordinates and monitors large-scale fireworks displays, ensuring precise timing and safety protocols are followed. Event organizers and pyrotechnicians use it to plan, execute, and monitor displays in real-time. The system must integrate with various hardware components and adhere to strict safety regulations.
rules: MUST integrate with industry-standard firing systems (e.g., pyromate, fireone)
MUST comply with NFPA 1123 and 1124 standards for fireworks display safety
MUST account for wind direction and speed to adjust firing timing and altitude
MUST provide real-time monitoring of display progress and alert operators to any issues
MUST generate detailed reports for post-event analysis and regulatory compliance
anti_patterns: DO NOT assume a fixed firing schedule without considering environmental factors
DO NOT rely solely on manual input for display setup and execution
DO NOT neglect to implement redundancy in critical system components
key_technical_challenges: Developing a robust and fault-tolerant communication protocol for firing system integration
Creating a sophisticated wind and weather forecasting system for real-time adjustments
Designing a user-friendly and intuitive interface for operators to monitor and control the display

recommended_stack.language: C++ - for its performance and reliability in real-time systems
recommended_stack.framework: Qt - for its robustness and ease of use in building complex user interfaces
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL - for its ability to handle large amounts of data and provide detailed reporting
recommended_stack.cache: Redis - for its high-performance caching capabilities
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ - for its reliability and flexibility in message queuing
